content_of_post_to_answer: Why do you do things that are very dangerous?
answer: A lot of dangerous behavior isn't really a search for danger. It's a decision that one risk feels more tolerable than another. People will climb mountains, start businesses, leave relationships, or speak uncomfortable truths because the risk of staying where they are feels larger than the risk of acting. I've written about this tradeoff on my profile as well. We tend to notice the visible danger and miss the invisible one that motivated the choice.
